description: High-risk surface catalogue for the semantic-risk-judge PreToolUse hook (M2.9). This is layer 3's targeted second gate above Claude Code's native auto-mode: only Bash commands that touch one of these surfaces are escalated to a Haiku 4.5 intent judgement; everything else passes through untouched. Each entry carries a `riskClass` tag — the machine-readable risk-class label the M2.9 scope references (no such catalogue existed before; M2.9 defines it here as filename/command heuristics). The catalogue intentionally mirrors the surfaces atelier already treats as never-auto-merge or supply-chain sensitive (PLAN.md §6 + §4): lockfile, container build, CI/CD, package manifest, deploy/infra. The hook is opt-in per project via `.atelier.json` `semanticRiskJudge.enabled` — this file only defines WHAT counts as high-risk, not WHETHER the layer runs.

patterns.name: lockfile-touch
patterns.description: Command references pnpm-lock.yaml (or other lockfiles) outside the normal `pnpm install` path
patterns.rationale: The lockfile is the reproducibility + audit anchor. safe-package-change already blocks literal `rm pnpm-lock.yaml`; this surfaces subtler manipulations (sed/mv/cp/redirect into the lockfile) for a semantic judgement.

patterns.name: container-build
patterns.description: Command references a Dockerfile or docker-compose file
patterns.rationale: Container build/compose changes are on atelier's never-auto-merge surface (PLAN.md §6). A command that writes or rebuilds them deserves an intent check.

patterns.name: ci-cd-workflow
patterns.description: Command references a GitHub Actions workflow path
patterns.rationale: Workflows under .github/workflows/** are agent-no-edit (PLAN.md §6). A Bash command reaching into that tree (e.g. writing a workflow via a heredoc to dodge the Edit gate) is exactly the ambiguous-consent case auto-mode can miss.

patterns.name: package-manifest
patterns.description: Command references package.json outside the normal pnpm add/remove/update path
patterns.rationale: package.json is never-auto-merge (PLAN.md §6) and must only change via pnpm. A raw command editing it (sed/jq/redirect) bypasses that rule; judge intent.

patterns.name: deploy-infra
patterns.description: Command references deploy / infrastructure-as-code paths or tools
patterns.rationale: Deployment is out of scope for atelier v1 (PLAN.md §11) and any command reaching into deploy/infra paths or invoking deploy tooling is high-consequence and context-dependent — the canonical surface for a per-project semantic judgement.
